message:
fix(scheduler): use CAS for counter saturation, validate queue tags, and recover gracefully from foreign-pinned waiters

GlobalInjector (src/runtime/scheduler/global_injector.rs):
- Replace load-then-fetch_sub pattern with fetch_update CAS loop for
  both decrement_pending_count and decrement_ready_count — the
  previous non-atomic check-then-subtract had a TOCTOU race where two
  concurrent decrementers could both observe count=1, both subtract,
  and wrap the u64 counter to u64::MAX, causing queue-depth heuristics
  to malfunction catastrophically
- Add concurrent_decrements_saturate_counters_at_zero stress test that
  runs 2000 iterations with 2 racing threads to verify saturation

IntrusiveRing/IntrusiveStack (src/runtime/scheduler/intrusive.rs):
- Add compile-time assertion that tag != 0 in both new() constructors
  since tag 0 is reserved for "not in any queue" sentinel — passing 0
  previously compiled silently but caused tasks to appear enqueued
  when they were not, leading to phantom task references
- Fix Default impl to use QUEUE_TAG_READY instead of tag 0
- Add tests: default_ring_uses_ready_tag, ring_rejects_zero_tag,
  stack_rejects_zero_tag

Worker (src/runtime/scheduler/worker.rs):
- Replace assert!/panic on foreign-pinned local waiter with tracing
  error log + skip — a local waiter pinned to a different worker than
  the one executing the completing task should not crash the entire
  scheduler; instead we log the anomaly and leave the waiter for its
  owning worker to pick up
- Add test_execute_ready_with_foreign_local_waiter_does_not_panic
  regression test
- Add explicit drop(guard) after panicking-task assertions

ThreeLane (src/runtime/scheduler/three_lane.rs):
- Add explicit drop(guard) calls after task setup in two test helpers
  to release the runtime state lock before triggering operations that
  re-acquire it

Registration (src/runtime/reactor/registration.rs):
- Simplify .map(f).unwrap_or(default) to .map_or(default, f)

State (src/runtime/state.rs):
- Minor formatting: collapse two-line BTreeMap binding to one line

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
